name: Neutron electric dipole moment below PSI limit
requirement: Any candidate framework must predict a neutron electric dipole moment |d_n| below the PSI 2020 upper limit. A nonzero d_n at observable levels would simultaneously signal CP violation beyond the SM and unambiguous time-reversal violation in the strong sector. The current bound directly tightens constraints on the QCD theta angle to |theta_QCD| < ~10^-10.

regime.description: Neutron-scale physics; sensitive to CP-violating new physics up to multi-TeV mass scales depending on couplings.

current_bound.reference: Abel et al. (nEDM), 'Measurement of the Permanent Electric Dipole Moment of the Neutron', PRL 124, 081803 (2020), arXiv:2001.11966

what_it_excludes: Severely constrains supersymmetric extensions with TeV-scale superpartners and natural CP phases (the 'SUSY CP problem'). Forbids many composite Higgs models with large CP-violating couplings. Together with the QCD theta term, motivates the existence of a Peccei-Quinn symmetry and an axion as the natural dynamical solution.

primary_references.citation: Abel et al. (nEDM Collaboration), 'Measurement of the Permanent Electric Dipole Moment of the Neutron', PRL 124, 081803 (2020)
Pospelov and Ritz, 'Electric dipole moments as probes of new physics', Annals Phys. 318, 119 (2005)
